steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The word "sheltering" modifies "roof" in a poem describing a barren, stormy landscape where no such roof exists. The context — heavy gray skies threatening a storm — tells us what function this roof would serve if it were present.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Protective": A roof's purpose in a storm is to shield from wind and rain. "Sheltering" in this context means offering protection from the elements, which matches "protective" exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — "Restrictive": Restrictive means limiting or confining. The poem presents a roof's absence as a source of vulnerability, not as something that would constrain anything. No element of the text supports this meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — "Affectionate": Affectionate means loving or tender. While the speaker does express love for the woman in the painting, the word "sheltering" describes the physical roof in the landscape, not any emotional quality. ✗
  • Choice D — "Suspicious": Suspicious means dubious or questionable. Nothing in the poem implies a roof would be untrustworthy; the absence of one simply leaves the scene exposed to the storm. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
